ops.title: daemonkit: Swift Testing CI hang — blocking socket I/O on cooperative executors
ops.body:
The daemonkit swift CI job hung for 4.9h (zero test output after `Build complete!`) on macos-26 while the suite passed locally in 0.12s across 300 stress iterations. Two independent sol diagnosis lanes converged on the mechanism.

**Root cause (leading, high confidence):** `SocketServerTests`' helper `sendLine` did blocking `connect`/`read` directly on Swift Testing's *cooperative executor* threads. Swift Testing runs tests in parallel; on a low-core runner several socket tests each park a cooperative worker in a blocking read, the server's non-overcommit `handlerQueue` blocks never get scheduled, and `stop()` then waits forever in `handlerGroup.wait()` — a stable deadlock. **Fleet rule: never do blocking I/O on Swift Testing task threads; serialize socket-heavy suites (`@Suite(.serialized)`) or move blocking I/O off the cooperative pool.**

**Secondary defect:** `SocketServer.stop()`'s self-pipe wake was one unchecked `write` — an EINTR loses the only wake for `poll(-1)` and `state = stopped` was committed first, so no retry was possible.

**Fixes shipped:** EINTR-retry + `precondition(written == 1)` on the shutdown pipe; SnapshotWatcher initial delivery moved into `setRegistrationHandler` (vnode watch provably armed before first callback — kills a rename-before-armed flake); socket suite `.serialized`; `SO_RCVTIMEO` result checked; monotonic `ContinuousClock` test deadlines; every test file wrapped in `@Suite(.timeLimit(.minutes(1)))` (minimum granularity is one minute; cancellation is cooperative and cannot unwind blocked C calls).

**CI hardening (same push):** `timeout-minutes` on every job (10 Go / 15 swift) so a wedge dies in minutes, not at GitHub's 6h kill; `cancel-in-progress` made PR-only — on main an in-progress run always finishes (the old policy repeatedly killed the slow swift job under rapid pushes, which is why no complete verdict existed since the Swift core landed). Caching was already optimal: setup-go `cache: true` + SPM `.build` cache put every job under 40s.
